data.title: Saudi Arabia Increases Crude Sales to China Following Price Cut
data.description: Saudi Arabia has increased its crude oil sales to China for March loading, following a significant price reduction. Bloomberg posted on X that the kingdom lowered the price of its primary oil grade for Asian buyers to the lowest point in over five years. This strategic move aims to boost demand from its top importer, China, amid fluctuating global oil markets. The price adjustment reflects Saudi Arabia's efforts to maintain its market share in Asia, a crucial region for its oil exports. The decision comes as part of broader strategies to navigate the challenges posed by global economic conditions and competitive pressures in the energy sector.

data.title: Philippine Fintech Firm Maya Considers $1 Billion US IPO
data.description: Philippine financial-technology company Maya is exploring the possibility of a US initial public offering (IPO) that could generate up to $1 billion. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Maya's interest in joining other Southeast Asian firms in listing their shares on international exchanges. This move reflects a growing trend among companies in the region to seek capital from overseas markets, aiming to expand their global presence and access a broader investor base. Maya's potential IPO underscores the increasing appeal of the US market for Southeast Asian businesses looking to scale their operations and enhance their financial standing.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin has fallen 22.3% year-to-date, potentially marking its worst first quarter performance since 2018.
data.description: Data shows that Bitcoin has fallen 22.3% since the beginning of the year, potentially marking its worst first quarter performance since 2018, when it fell 49.7%. Bitcoin started the year trading at approximately $87,700 and has now fallen by about $20,000 to around $68,000. Furthermore, Bitcoin may be poised for its first consecutive month of declines in January and February. Analyst Daan Trades Crypto, writing on the X platform, stated that the first quarter is known for its volatility, and historically, events in the first quarter typically do not carry over into later periods. Nick Ruck, director at LVRG Research, stated that the continued decline in Bitcoin's price reflects a normal correction phase rather than a structural collapse in the asset's long-term trajectory. Historical patterns show that Bitcoin's resilience often leads to strong recoveryes in the following months. ETH has fallen 34.3% so far this quarter. Bitcoin fell 2.3% in the past 24 hours, trading at $68,670.

data.title: A whale deposited 732,000 VVV tokens into Coinbase and made a profit of $1.77 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, in the past 24 hours, a whale (0x953...2b96) deposited 732,481 VVV tokens into Coinbase, worth $3.03 million, and made a profit of $1.77 million. This whale initially withdrew 763,725 VVV tokens from Coinbase, worth $1.56 million, then staked them for 6 months, ultimately depositing a total of 849,500 VVV tokens into Coinbase, worth $3.33 million.

data.title: DeFi Spot Trading Volume Triples Since Start of 2026
data.description:
Decentralized finance spot trading activity has surged sharply in the opening weeks of 2026, with total weekly volume rising nearly threefold compared to early January levels. The latest data shows aggregate DeFi spot volume climbing from roughly the low-$20 billion range in early January to nearly $80 billion by early February.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/defi-spot-trading-volume-triples-since-start-of-2026/

data.title: EU Banks Witness Surge in Cross-Border Mergers Amid Profit and Stock Price Rise
data.description: Cross-border merger activity among EU banks has reached its highest level since the 2008 financial crisis, driven by increased profits and rising stock prices. According to Jin10, the Financial Times reports that last year saw several billion-euro bank merger deals, pushing the total value of European cross-border banking transactions to €17 billion, up from €3.4 billion the previous year. Policymakers have long advocated for a more integrated EU banking market. However, bank executives note that regulatory hurdles and political resistance have hindered progress, allowing larger U.S. counterparts to surpass them. Despite slow progress in reducing bureaucratic barriers within the EU banking sector, there are signs that European banks are continuing to pursue cross-border expansion deals.

data.title: Kimi Launches Beta Version of Kimi Claw with AI Integration
data.description:
Kimi has officially launched the beta version of Kimi Claw, according to BlockBeats. This new service offers cloud-native integration based on the open-source AI Agent framework, OpenClaw. Users can operate the AI Agent directly from a browser tab without needing to install or configure a server locally, ensuring 24/7 online availability. Additionally, it allows for bridging with local or third-party OpenClaw instances.

The Kimi Claw Beta is built on Kimi K2.5 and incorporates a practical skills library to enable long-term memory and autonomous operations. Currently, access is limited to Allegretto members and above.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Iran's Foreign Minister to Engage in Technical Discussions with IAEA
data.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi announced plans to hold in-depth technical discussions with Rafael Grossi, Director General of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA), on Monday. According to Jin10, these talks are set to precede diplomatic negotiations with the United States scheduled for Tuesday. Araghchi also plans to meet with Oman's Foreign Minister during his visit to Geneva. He emphasized that he is arriving with practical proposals aimed at reaching a fair and just agreement, while ruling out the option of succumbing to threats in the negotiations.

data.title: Animoca Brands Secures VASP License from Dubai's VARA
data.description: Animoca Brands has announced that it has obtained a Virtual Asset Service Provider (VASP) license from the Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA) in Dubai. According to Foresight News, this license permits Animoca Brands to conduct business within the Emirate of Dubai, excluding the Dubai International Financial Centre, and to offer virtual asset brokerage, trading, management, and investment services to global institutional and qualified investors. VARA, established under Dubai Law No. 4 of 2022, is the authoritative body responsible for regulating, supervising, and overseeing virtual assets and related activities within the Emirate of Dubai.

data.title: South Korean Shipping Firm Dominates Tanker Market
data.description: A South Korean shipping company has secured a significant portion of the tanker market, bolstered by the involvement of one of the industry's wealthiest individuals. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the firm's strategic maneuvers that have led to its dominant position. The company's growth is attributed to its ability to leverage industry connections and capitalize on market opportunities. This expansion has positioned the firm as a key player in the global shipping industry, reflecting broader trends in maritime trade and logistics. The involvement of influential figures in the industry has further strengthened its market presence, underscoring the importance of strategic partnerships in achieving business success.

data.title: Early ETH investors who had been dormant for 10.6 years have awakened, with their $443 investment now worth $2.81 million.
data.description: According to on-chain data, an early ETH investor awoke after 10.6 years of dormancy and attempted to transfer 1 ETH to Gemini, but the transaction failed. The 1430 ETH invested for $443 during the ICO are now worth $2.81 million, a return of 6335 times.
